summary: Excellent audience (AUD) recording of a legendary Saratoga show where the Scarlet‑Fire jam and an epic Morning Dew stole the night.
review: Fans rave that this June‑1983 SPAC performance is one of the band’s most electrifying, highlighted by a blistering Scarlet > Fire sequence, a thunderous The Wheel, and a transcendent Morning Dew.  The Jeff Silberman audience tape from the balcony captures the energy with surprisingly clear sound for an AUD source, though a few listeners note occasional muddiness.  Overall both the music and the recording are regarded as outstanding.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews uniformly praise the musical performance (especially Scarlet‑Fire, Morning Dew and The Wheel) and rate the audience recording as good to excellent; weighted average yields a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Balcony audience tape recorded digitally by Jeff Silberman; many cite crispness and low crowd noise, while a few mention occasional muddiness or ear‑fatiguing sections.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid, but the second set is legendary – a nonstop surge of jam power that builds from Scarlet‑Fire through Space and culminates in an epic Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for incendiary guitar work on Scarlet‑Fire and Morning Dew; described as ‘unleashed’, ‘perfect tone’ and a highlight of the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for massive low‑end bass bombs, especially during The Wheel and Dew; fans call his six‑string bass performance “monster”.
band_member_comments.Bob: Earns a gold‑star solo before the drums on several accounts; jam into drums highlighted as a high point.
band_member_comments.Drums: Percussion in Scarlet‑Fire described as tribal, with powerful space/drum sections; overall drum work considered solid though some note occasional looseness.
